breakdown: The PR demonstrates strong naming conventions with descriptive test names and function names that clearly convey purpose (e.g., `test_multiclass_sums_all_levels`). Error handling is solid with try/except guards on all public functions returning safe fallback values, though some edge cases could use more specific error types. Type safety is decent with return type annotations present, but the game_state parameter lacks a TypedDict definition which would improve compile-time safety. Architecture is clean with good separation of concerns; private helpers are appropriately named with underscore prefixes. Test coverage has some gaps—several test functions appear truncated mid-definition, and some edge case assertions are missing from the test file. Documentation quality is high on public functions with D&D 5e rule references, but private helpers lack docstrings.
reasoning: Naming is excellent and nearly perfect with only minor room for improvement. Error handling catches the right exceptions but could use typed errors for debugging. Type safety is good but would benefit from TypedDict definitions for game_state structures. Architecture follows good practices with single-responsibility functions and proper module organization. Test coverage is slightly below ideal because several test cases appear incomplete (truncated assertions, empty function bodies). Documentation is strong on public interfaces but omits docstrings on the key private helper `_total_character_level` which contains the core bug fix.
